---
title: "Quarto 3 — Figures & Visualizations"
subtitle: "Publication-quality plots in .qmd"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Goals — 45 minutes

::: {.incremental}
- Basic ggplot → publication-quality
- Figure sizing with chunk options
- Multi-panel via `patchwork`
- Interactive via `plotly`
- Side-by-side via Quarto columns
- Save for manuscripts
:::

# Sizing — the R Markdown → Quarto gotcha {.section-divider}

## Names changed

| R Markdown | Quarto |
|------------|--------|
| `fig.width=8` | `fig-width: 8` |
| `fig.height=4` | `fig-height: 4` |
| `fig.cap="..."` | `fig-cap: "..."` |
| `out.width="80%"` | `out-width: "80%"` |
| `fig.align="center"` | `fig-align: "center"` |

::: {.fragment}
**Dot becomes hyphen.** That's the rule.
:::

## fig-width vs out-width

::: {.incremental}
- `fig-width` — *creation* dimensions in inches
- `out-width` — *display* dimensions in the rendered doc
- Use both for: "render at 8×6 inches but show at 60% width"
:::

# patchwork {.section-divider}

## Combine plots

```{.r}
p1 <- ggplot(biomarker_data, aes(pfoa_serum)) +
  geom_histogram()
p2 <- ggplot(biomarker_data, aes(pfoa_serum, cholesterol)) +
  geom_point()

p1 + p2          # side by side
p1 / p2          # stacked
(p1 + p2) / p3   # two on top, one below
```

::: {.fragment}
Arithmetic operators on plot objects. Easy.
:::

## Auto-tagged panels

```{.r}
p1 + p2 + p3 +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
```

::: {.fragment}
Panels labeled A, B, C automatically.
:::

# Quarto columns {.section-divider}

## Side-by-side plots in prose

```markdown
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
[plot chunk 1]
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
[plot chunk 2]
:::

::::
```

::: {.fragment}
Each plot retains its own caption and label. Quarto-only — R Markdown doesn't have this.
:::

# Interactive with plotly {.section-divider}

## ggplot → plotly

```{.r}
p <- ggplot(biomarker_data,
            aes(pfoa_serum, cholesterol, color = sex,
                text = paste("Age:", age))) +
  geom_point()

ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("text", "x", "y"))
```

::: {.incremental}
- Hover, zoom, pan
- HTML only — degrades in Word/PDF
- For multi-format: conditional with `knitr::is_html_output()`
:::

# Saving figures {.section-divider}

## ggsave for manuscripts

```{.r}
# For journal submission
ggsave("figures/figure1.png",
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Or TIFF if required
ggsave("figures/figure1.tiff",
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300,
       compression = "lzw")

# Or vector
ggsave("figures/figure1.svg",
       width = 7, height = 5)
```

# Cross-references {.section-divider}

## Two requirements

::: {.incremental}
1. **Label with prefix** — `fig-`, `tbl-`, `eq-`, `sec-`, `lst-`
2. **Caption** — `fig-cap:` or `tbl-cap:`
:::

## In prose

```markdown
The data are positively associated
(@fig-pfoa-cholesterol), consistent
across sites (@fig-faceted).
```

::: {.fragment}
Renders as "Figure 1" and "Figure 5" with clickable links. Numbering automatic.
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Notebook Quarto 3

::: {.callout-tip}
Open `afternoon_quarto/03_figures_and_visualizations.qmd`. Exercises 3.1, 3.2, 3.3.
:::

45 minutes. Then equations + citations.
